info.title: RelayMock REST API
info.description: Local mock for a Pushbullet-like device relay API. It deliberately uses development-only authentication and local storage.

get.summary: Describe mock features, limits, and supported transports
Return authenticated storage usage and pressure

post.summary: Create a local mock account and its first device
post.description:
Development-only bootstrap endpoint.

Production should replace this with passkeys/OAuth and an approved device-link flow.

201.description: Local account and first device created.

Cache-Control.description: Prevents storage of the plaintext device token.

Pragma.description: Legacy cache prevention for the plaintext device token.

get.summary: Get public Passkey and Turnstile configuration

post.summary: Create a one-use Passkey registration challenge
Verify a Passkey registration and issue a browser session

201.description: Passkey account and browser session created.

post.summary: Create a one-use Passkey authentication challenge
Verify a Passkey assertion and issue a browser session
Rotate the current browser session and CSRF token

200.description: Session rotated; the previous cookie and CSRF token are invalid.
Browser sessions for the current account.

201.description: One-use link created; the plaintext token is returned only once.
Device and device-scoped Bearer token issued.

200.description: Device-link status without the plaintext token.

schema.title: Response List Devices V1 Devices Get

post.description: Mock an already-approved device link and return a device-scoped token.

201.description: Device linked and device-scoped token issued.

Cache-Control.description: Prevents storage of the plaintext device token.

Pragma.description: Legacy cache prevention for the plaintext device token.

200.description: Idempotent replay of an existing push.

Idempotent-Replayed.description: Always true when an existing push is returned for the same request.

put.description: Local stand-in for a short-lived presigned R2 PUT URL.

post.description: A Service Worker uses the short-lived delivery token from a Web Push payload. A cached acknowledgement is valid only after its IndexedDB transaction commits.

200.description: Delivery state updated or idempotently replayed.

get.description:
Local stand-in for a short-lived presigned R2 GET URL.

Like an object-store signed URL, it is reusable until its expiry.

schema.title: Response List Subscriptions V1 Web Push Subscriptions Get
Response Run Cleanup V1 Mock Cleanup Post
Response Reset Mock V1 Mock Reset Post

PushCreate.title: PushCreate
PushCreate.description: Six mutually exclusive request shapes represented as OpenAPI ``oneOf``.

PushTarget.title: PushTarget
PushTarget.description: Named discriminated union used by both requests and responses.

DeliveryBearer.description: Short-lived token scoped to one file delivery ledger row.

XRequestID.description: Request identifier echoed in server logs and error details.

BadRequest.description: The request cursor or other syntax is invalid.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, expired, or revoked device token.

Forbidden.description: Ticket or administrative credential is invalid.

NotFound.description: Resource does not exist or belongs to another user.

Conflict.description: Current resource state prevents the operation.

PayloadTooLarge.description: File or payload exceeds the configured limit.

UnprocessableContent.description: Uploaded bytes fail size, digest, or state verification.

InternalError.description: The local mock encountered an unexpected error.

InsufficientStorage.description: Storage pressure prevents accepting the upload.
